guide.addLocation.title: Add Location Guide
guide.addLocation.step1: Fill in mandatory store info: Name (*), Phone (*), Email (*). Store ID is auto-generated by the system.
guide.addLocation.step2: Complete Address (*) and select Province/Kota/Kecamatan/Desa from dropdowns. Postal Code auto-fills. Add Detail Location for landmarks (optional).
guide.addLocation.step3: Upload a Store Photo (*) - required for new stores. Format: JPG/PNG, max 2MB. The map picker below helps you pinpoint the store location.
guide.addLocation.step4: Set Category (Main Branch/Branch/Warehouse/Office). Pick Manager from employee data - click the field to open employee list and select one.
guide.addLocation.step5: Configure Operating Hours (weekly), Social Media links, and toggle Active/Inactive status. Active stores can process transactions.
guide.addLocation.step6: Save via 'Simpan Lokasi' or save as Draft. System validates required fields before submitting.
guide.addCategory.title: Add Category Guide
guide.addCategory.step1: Enter Category Name (*) - This is the main identifier that will appear in product lists, filters, and reports. Use clear, descriptive names like 'Beverages', 'Electronics', or 'Dairy Products'. This name is what you'll select later when adding products.
guide.addCategory.step2: Add Description (optional) - Provide details about what products belong in this category. This helps staff understand categorization and appears in detailed views. Example: 'All cold beverages including coffee-based and non-coffee drinks'.
guide.addCategory.step3: Select Icon or Upload Image - Choose from preset icons or upload a custom image. This visual identifier appears in category lists and helps with quick recognition at the cashier and product pages.
guide.addCategory.step4: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active categories can be assigned to products. Inactive categories are hidden from product forms but retain existing product assignments.
guide.addCategory.step5: After saving, this category will be available in the Add Product form under the Category dropdown. Go to Add Product to create products under this category. Categories are also visible in the cashier panel for filtering products by group.
guide.addSupplier.title: Add Supplier Guide
guide.addSupplier.step1: Enter Supplier Company Name (*) - This is the business entity that supplies your products. It will appear in purchase orders, stock reports, and supplier lists.
guide.addSupplier.step2: Enter Contact Person - The name of the person responsible at the supplier company. This person will be referenced in purchase orders and communication logs.
guide.addSupplier.step3: Complete Phone Number and Email - Contact details for placing orders, negotiating prices, or resolving delivery issues. These will be available in the supplier detail view.
guide.addSupplier.step4: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active suppliers are available for purchase orders. Inactive suppliers are preserved in history but cannot be used for new orders. Click Save to register the supplier in the system.
guide.addProduct.title: Add Product Guide
guide.addProduct.step1: Select Store (Super Admin only) - If you are Super Admin, choose one or more stores for this product. Each selected store will have this product listed. If NO store exists yet, click the 'Add Store' button to create one first. For non-Super Admin, the product is automatically added to your assigned store.
guide.addProduct.step2: Enter Product Name (*) & Basic Info - This is the product name that appears on receipts, sales reports, and inventory. Fill optional fields: Brand, Barcode, SKU, Unit (default pcs), Description. The barcode/SKU helps with tracking and quick scanning at the cashier.
guide.addProduct.step3: Select Category (*) & Supplier - Choose a Category from the dropdown. If no category exists yet, go to Category Management first to create one. Categories help organize products in the cashier panel and reports. Select Supplier and Tax (Super Admin only). These connect the product to its vendor for purchase orders.
guide.addProduct.step4: Set Pricing & Stock - Selling Price (*) is required. Cost Price helps track profitability. Stock quantity sets initial inventory. Min Stock triggers low-stock alerts. Points can be assigned for loyalty rewards. For batch tracking, enable 'Has Batch' to manage expiry dates and batch numbers.
guide.addProduct.step5: Variants & Modifiers - Enable 'Has Variant' for products with variations (e.g., size Small/Medium/Large, color Red/Blue). Each variant can have its own price and stock. Enable 'Has Modifier' for add-ons (e.g., extra cheese, topping). These appear in the cashier when the product is selected.
guide.addProduct.step6: Upload Product Image - Visual identification for the product (PNG, JPG, WEBP up to 2MB). Appears in the cashier product grid and customer receipts. Set Status (Active/Inactive) and Availability. Active products are available for sale.
guide.addProduct.step7: Save - Review all information. Click 'Next' through all 3 steps, then click 'Simpan' to save. You can also save as Draft to complete later. After saving, the product will be available in the cashier for checkout and tracked in inventory.
guide.addMember.title: Add Member Guide
guide.addMember.step1: Enter Full Name (*) - The customer's identity for the loyalty program. This appears on member cards, point transactions, and sales history.
guide.addMember.step2: Enter Phone Number (*) - Used for member lookup at checkout and communication. Members can be searched by phone number during sales.
guide.addMember.step3: Complete additional data: Birth Date, Gender, and Address. These enable birthday promotions and demographic analysis in reports.
guide.addMember.step4: Select Membership Tier - If NO tiers have been created yet, the dropdown will be empty. Go to Member Tier first to create tiers (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum). If tiers exist, select one to determine the member's benefits and discount level. Initial Points can be added as a welcome bonus. Click Save to register the member.
guide.addMemberTier.title: Add Member Tier Guide
guide.addMemberTier.step1: Set Tier Name (*) - Enter a unique name like 'Silver', 'Gold', or 'Platinum'. This is the level displayed on member profiles, cards, and receipts. Higher tiers should offer better benefits.
guide.addMemberTier.step2: Set Minimum Points (*) - The number of loyalty points required to reach this tier. Points are accumulated from customer purchases. Each tier must have a higher minimum than the previous level.
guide.addMemberTier.step3: Set Discount Percentage (*) - The discount amount members at this tier receive on purchases. Automatically applied at checkout for eligible products. Higher tiers typically get larger discounts.
guide.addMemberTier.step4: Visual Identity - Choose a badge Icon (Star, Premium, Medal, Diamond, Plus) and Color for this tier. The badge appears on member profiles, lists, and receipts for quick recognition.
guide.addMemberTier.step5: Benefits & Perks - List the advantages members get at this level (e.g., 'Free delivery', 'Priority service', 'Double points weekend'). These perks are shown on the member's profile.
guide.addMemberTier.step6: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active tiers are available for member assignments. Inactive tiers cannot be selected when creating/editing members but retain existing member data.
guide.addMemberTier.step7: Save & Chain to Members - Click Save to create the tier. After saving, go to Add Member to assign this tier to customers. Members will earn points and receive discounts based on their assigned tier.
guide.addStockOpname.title: Add Stock Opname Guide
guide.addStockOpname.step1: Set Audit Date (*) and Auditor Name (*) - Choose the audit date and enter the person responsible for the stock opname. This information identifies when and who performed the inventory check.
guide.addStockOpname.step2: Upload Excel or Download Template - Use the Download Template button to get a pre-formatted Excel file. Fill it with your inventory data offline. When you Upload Excel, the data is parsed and shown in the table first for review before saving. You can edit rows, fix data, and then save. For large inventories, this is faster than manual entry.
guide.addStockOpname.step3: Fill Item Rows - Each row represents one product. Enter Product Code, Product Name (*), Unit (e.g., pcs, kg), and Location. Click the search icon to find products from the existing product list. Use +Add Product to add more rows.
guide.addStockOpname.step4: Enter Stock Data - For each item, fill: Opening Stock (stok awal), Incoming Items (barang masuk since last check), Outgoing Items (barang keluar). The system calculates Ending Stock (persediaan akhir) = Opening + Incoming - Outgoing.
guide.addStockOpname.step5: Enter Physical Stock & View Difference - Fill the Physical Stock (stok fisik) column with the actual count from your physical inventory. The system automatically calculates the Difference (selisih = fisik - ending stock). Positive = surplus, Negative = shortage.
guide.addStockOpname.step6: Add Notes - Add per-item notes (keterangan) in each row explaining any discrepancies. Add overall audit notes (catatan) in the text area at the bottom. Notes help explain stock differences during review.
guide.addStockOpname.step7: Save - Click 'Simpan & Selesai' to complete the opname (automatically marked as Completed). Use 'Simpan sebagai Draft' to save progress and finish later. The opname will appear in the Stock Opname list.
guide.addDepartment.title: Add Department Guide
guide.addDepartment.step1: Enter Department Name (*) - The name of the unit or division within your company (e.g., 'Kitchen', 'Finance', 'Marketing'). This department will be selectable when creating Positions.
guide.addDepartment.step2: Add Description (optional) - Describe the function, responsibilities, and scope of this department. Example: 'Handles all food preparation and kitchen operations.' This helps staff understand the department's role.
guide.addDepartment.step3: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active departments are available for position assignments. Inactive departments are hidden from position forms but retain existing position data.
guide.addDepartment.step4: Save - Click Save to create the department. After saving, go to Add Position to assign positions (e.g., 'Head Chef', 'Cashier Supervisor') under this department. Departments organize job roles in the company structure.
guide.addPosition.title: Add Position Guide
guide.addPosition.step1: Enter Position Name (*) - The job title or role name (e.g., 'Senior Cashier', 'Head Chef', 'Store Supervisor'). This position will be assigned to employees and appears on employee profiles and reports.
guide.addPosition.step2: Select Department (*) - Choose the department this position belongs to. This is MANDATORY. If NO departments exist yet, a message will appear with a button 'Add Department' to create one first. The department dropdown shows all active departments from the Department Management page. This links the position to its organizational unit for reporting and structure.
guide.addPosition.step3: Add Description (optional) - Describe the responsibilities, skills required, and scope of this position. Example: 'Responsible for managing daily cash register operations and customer payments.' This helps HR and managers understand the role.
guide.addPosition.step4: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active positions can be assigned to employees. Inactive positions are hidden from employee forms but retain existing employee assignments.
guide.addPosition.step5: Save - Click Save to create the position. After saving, go to Employee Management to assign this position to employees. Employees with this position will have their role and department information recorded in the system.
guide.addEmployee.title: Add Employee Guide
guide.addEmployee.step1: Fill Personal Information (*) - Enter Full Name, Username, Email, Password, Phone Number, Place of Birth, Address, Gender, and Date of Birth. The Employee ID is auto-generated. These details identify the employee and are used for login credentials.
guide.addEmployee.step2: Select Department & Position - Choose Department from existing departments (created in Department Management). Then select a Position that belongs to that department (created in Position Management). If no departments exist, create them first. This links the employee to their organizational role.
guide.addEmployee.step3: Select Store & Shift - Choose the Store/Location where the employee works. Then select their Shift (depends on the selected store - shifts must be created in Shift Management first). If no stores exist, create one in Store Management first.
guide.addEmployee.step4: Set Employment Type & Salary - Choose type: Full Time, Part Time, Contract, or Internship. For Contract/Internship, set Start Date and Duration - the End Date is auto-calculated. Enter Monthly Salary; the Daily Salary is auto-calculated for payroll.
guide.addEmployee.step5: Upload Photo & Documents - Upload a profile photo (JPG, PNG, max 2MB). Upload employee documents (PDF, DOC, XLS, JPG, PNG - multiple files). These files are stored and visible in the employee detail page.
guide.addEmployee.step6: Set Account & Status - Select Role (default: User) and optional Additional Access Menu for permissions. Toggle Active/Inactive status. Active employees can log in and access the system. Inactive employees retain data but cannot log in.
guide.addEmployee.step7: Save - Review all information. Click 'Save' to register the employee. Use 'Save as Draft' to complete later. After saving, the employee appears in Employee List and can be assigned to shifts, sales, and store operations.
guide.addTax.title: Add Tax Guide
guide.addTax.step1: Enter Tax Name (*) - The display name for this tax configuration (e.g., 'PPN 11%', 'PPh 0.5%'). This name appears when selecting tax in the Add Product form and on receipts.
guide.addTax.step2: Select Tax Type (*) - Choose from: PPN (Pajak Pertambahan Nilai / Value Added Tax), PPh (Pajak Penghasilan / Income Tax), or Non-Pajak (Non-Tax). Determines how the tax is calculated and reported.
guide.addTax.step3: Set Tax Rate (*) - The percentage rate for this tax (e.g., 11 for 11%, 0.5 for 0.5%). This rate is automatically applied to product prices when the tax is selected in the product form.
guide.addTax.step4: Add Description (optional) - Provide details about this tax, its regulations, or applicable products. Example: 'PPN 11% sesuai UU HPP untuk semua produk makanan & minuman.'
guide.addTax.step5: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active taxes appear in the Add Product form for selection. Inactive taxes are hidden from selection but retain existing product assignments.
guide.addTax.step6: Save & Chain to Products - Click Save to create the tax. After saving, go to Add/Edit Product to apply this tax to products. The tax will be automatically calculated on the selling price at checkout and appear on receipts. Connected to sales reports for tax reporting.

This section displays 5 summary cards — Revenue (today's earnings + trend), Orders (total transactions), Active Products, Member count, and Low Stock (highlighted red when items need restocking). These cards are read-only; the chart and orders sections below contain the interactive buttons (Download Report, View All, Eye, Printer).

guide.support.s24cdesc: Call our customer support hotline for urgent issues. Available Monday-Friday 08:00-17:00 WIB.

guide.addLocation.s1desc: Enter your store's basic details: store name, store ID (auto-generated), phone number, and email address. Ensure all fields marked with * are filled correctly.
guide.addLocation.s2desc: Fill in the full address: select province, city, district, and village from dropdowns. The postal code will be auto-filled based on your selection. You can also add additional location details in the 'Detail Location' field.
guide.addLocation.s3desc: Upload a clear photo of your store (JPG or PNG, max 2MB) to enhance visual recognition in the system. Click the upload area or drag & drop your image.
guide.addLocation.s4desc: Activate or deactivate your store using the toggle switch. Active stores can process transactions, while inactive stores are temporarily disabled.
guide.addLocation.s5desc: Review all entered information, then click the 'Simpan Lokasi' button to save. The system will validate your data before storing it in the database.
guide.dailyReport.s25adesc: View daily business summary including total transactions, revenue, HPP, and profit. Filter by date range.
guide.pnl.s25bdesc: View profit and loss statement showing revenue, costs, and net profit for any period.
guide.cashflow.s25cdesc: Monitor cash inflows from sales (cash, QRIS, transfer) and outflows for financial management.
guide.shift.s26adesc: Manage work shifts. Create morning, afternoon, or night shifts. Shifts are assigned to employees and used in transaction reporting.
guide.shift.s26bdesc: Click the Add button to create a new shift. Fill in the shift name, type (daily/weekly), start & end time, date range, and assign employees.
guide.shift.s26cdesc: View all created shifts in the table. Each shift shows its schedule, assigned employees, and status. You can edit or delete shifts from here.
guide.sales.s28adesc: View detailed sales transactions history. Filter by date, store, payment method. Export data for accounting purposes.
guide.bestselling.s28bdesc: See which products sell the most. Analyze sales volume and revenue to identify top-performing items for inventory and marketing decisions.
guide.payment.s26bdesc: Manage payment methods available at checkout: Cash, QRIS, Bank Transfer. These appear in the cashier payment selection.
guide.addShift.title: Add Shift Guide
guide.addShift.step1: Enter Shift Name (*) - The name for this work shift (e.g., 'Shift Pagi', 'Shift Malam'). This appears in employee assignment and transaction reports.
guide.addShift.step2: Set Start Time (*) and End Time (*) - Define the working hours for this shift. Used to track which shift processed each transaction.
guide.addShift.step3: Add Description (optional) - Additional notes about this shift, such as team members or special instructions.
guide.addShift.step4: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active shifts are available for employee assignment and appear in shift selection.
guide.addShift.step5: Save & Chain to Employees - After saving, go to Employee Management to assign this shift to employees. Transactions will be grouped by shift for reporting.
guide.addTypePayment.title: Add Payment Method Guide
guide.addTypePayment.step1: Enter Payment Name (*) - The display name (e.g., 'Tunai', 'QRIS', 'Transfer Bank'). This appears in the cashier payment selection screen.
guide.addTypePayment.step2: Select Type (*) - Choose from: Tunai (cash), Non-Tunai (cashless), or Transfer. Determines how the payment is categorized in cash flow reports.
guide.addTypePayment.step3: Add Description (optional) - Details about this payment method, such as fees or processing time.
guide.addTypePayment.step4: Set Status (Active/Inactive) - Active methods appear in cashier checkout. Inactive methods are hidden from selection.
guide.addTypePayment.step5: Save & Chain to Transactions - After saving, this payment method will be available in the cashier checkout modal. All transactions using this method will be tracked in sales reports and cash flow.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.title: Create Purchase Order Guide
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step1: Select or add Supplier - Search for existing suppliers or type a new supplier name to add it directly.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step2: Select PIC (*) - Search and select the employee responsible for this purchase order.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step3: Set PO Date and Time (*) - Choose the order date and time.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step4: Add Items - Enter the name of the raw material/item to order, quantity, and unit price.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step5: Auto-calculated total - The system calculates the total based on qty x price for each item.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step6: Add Notes (optional) - Notes for the supplier or shipping information.
guide.addPurchaseOrder.step7: Save PO - Click Save PO to create the purchase order. Initial status: Pending.
guide.addIngredient.title: Add Ingredient Guide
guide.addIngredient.step1: Ingredient Name (*) - Enter the name of the ingredient or raw material.
guide.addIngredient.step2: Category & Supplier - Optionally set a category and default supplier for this ingredient.
guide.addIngredient.step3: Unit Conversion - Set purchase unit, base unit (smallest unit), and conversion factor (e.g. 1 kg = 1000 gram).
guide.addIngredient.step4: Stock & Min Stock - Set initial stock quantity and minimum stock threshold for low stock alerts.
guide.addIngredient.step5: Cost Price - Enter the purchase cost price per unit.
guide.addIngredient.step6: Status (Active/Inactive) - Active ingredients appear in purchase order item search.
guide.addIngredient.step7: Save - Click Save to create the ingredient. It will be available in PO item search.
guide.stock.s27adesc: Perform stock opname to reconcile physical stock with system records. Upload Excel or fill manually, then save to update inventory.
guide.stock.s27bdesc: View all stock changes: new products, sales, purchases, adjustments, and transfers between stores.
guide.stock.s27cdesc: Monitor products with stock below minimum threshold. Use this to plan reorders and prevent stockouts.

guide.menu.guideDesc: Browse all pages and their descriptions
guide.page.cashier.desc: Point of Sale page for processing customer transactions, selecting products, applying discounts, and completing payments with multiple payment methods.
guide.page.purchaseOrder.desc: Manage purchase orders to suppliers. Create, view, and track orders for inventory replenishment.
guide.page.ingredient.desc: Manage raw materials and ingredients inventory. Track stock levels, costs, and unit conversions for purchasing.
guide.page.addPurchaseOrder.desc: Create a new purchase order by selecting supplier, adding products with quantities and prices, and submitting for fulfillment.
guide.page.addIngredient.desc: Add a new ingredient or raw material with name, unit, unit conversion, initial stock, and cost price.
guide.page.cashRegister.desc: Current cash register session showing opening balance, total sales, payment breakdown, and session management.
guide.page.cashRegister.openClose.desc: Open or close the cash register for a shift. Enter opening balance when starting and reconcile when ending.
guide.page.cashRegister.history.desc: View past cash register sessions with opening/closing balances, discrepancies, and transaction summaries.
guide.page.discount.desc: Manage discount rules: percentage or fixed amount discounts with conditions, validity periods, and applicable products.
guide.page.addDiscount.desc: Create a new discount with name, type (percentage/nominal), value, validity period, and applicable products or categories.
guide.page.kitchenDisplay.desc: Kitchen Display System - view incoming orders in real-time, mark items as being prepared or ready for serving.
guide.page.productionOrder.desc: Production orders for manufacturing or preparing products. Track orders from creation to completion.
guide.page.addProductionOrder.desc: Create a new production order by selecting products, specifying quantities, production date, and assigned production team.
guide.page.bom.desc: Bill of Materials - define product recipes with raw materials, quantities, and production costs for manufacturing.
guide.page.addBom.desc: Create a new Bill of Materials by selecting the finished product, listing raw material components with quantities and unit costs.
guide.page.goodsReceipt.desc: Record receipt of goods from suppliers. Match against purchase orders and update inventory automatically.
guide.page.addGoodsReceipt.desc: Create a goods receipt by selecting a purchase order and confirming quantities received. Inventory is updated automatically.
guide.page.salesReturn.desc: Process customer returns of sold products. View return history and reconcile inventory and payments.
guide.page.purchaseReturn.desc: Process returns to suppliers for defective or incorrect goods. Track return status and credit/refund information.
guide.page.stockTransfer.desc: Transfer stock between store locations. Track outgoing and incoming transfers with status updates.
guide.page.addStockTransfer.desc: Create a stock transfer by selecting source and destination stores, products, and quantities to transfer.
guide.page.expenseCategory.desc: Manage expense categories used to classify and track business expenditures for financial reporting.
guide.page.expense.desc: Record and manage business expenses. Track spending by category, approve or reject expense requests.
guide.page.pricePerStore.desc: Configure product prices per store location. Set different selling prices for the same product across different branches.
guide.page.table.desc: Manage restaurant tables: create, edit, View, or delete tables. Update table status (available, occupied, reserved).
guide.page.reservation.desc: Manage table reservations. Create, edit, or cancel reservations with customer details, date, time, and party size.
guide.page.addRole.desc: Create and manage user roles with granular permissions. Define what each role can access and perform in the system.
guide.page.notification.desc: View system notifications including low stock alerts, new orders, and important system updates.
guide.page.profile.desc: View and edit your user profile including personal information, email, and account settings.
guide.page.customerOrder.desc: Customer self-ordering interface. Browse menu, place orders, and track order status from the customer's perspective.
guide.page.customerDisplay.desc: Customer-facing display showing order details, queue status, and payment information during checkout.
guide.page.storeGeospatial.desc: View all store locations on an interactive map. Visualize geographical distribution and store information.
guide.page.adminList.desc: Manage admin user accounts. View, add, or remove administrators who have access to the system backend.
guide.page.addAdmin.desc: Create a new admin user account by entering personal details, login credentials, and role assignment.

page.backup.restoreNote: Restore is done per-entity through their respective pages:
page.backup.restoreHint: Upload the backed-up Excel file to each entity's page using the Import Excel feature.
page.backup.tips: Backup regularly, especially before making bulk changes.
Store backup files in a safe place (cloud storage, external drive).
Backup is point-in-time — create a new one whenever master data changes.
One backup file covers 12 master data entities at once.
To restore, open the respective entity page and use the Import Excel button.
